name: GitHub PR (real handler — open PR via GitHub App)

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ title: $('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.title, head: $('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.head, base: ($('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.base || $env['GITHUB_BASE_BRANCH'] || 'main'), body: $('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.body || '', draft: !!$('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.draft }) }}

_comment: Real handler. Pattern mirrors create-adr.json (PR #11): webhook → HMAC validate (R-02 fail-closed) → Respond 200 in parallel → ADR-0003 sign → Call Skills Router → Routing Gate IF (skill==='github-pr' && status!=='pending_approval') → GitHub-App branch (Build JWT → Get Installation Token → Open PR) → Telegram reply. Required env vars: GITHUB_PR_WEBHOOK_SECRET, SKILLS_ROUTER_URL, SKILLS_ROUTER_SECRET, GITHUB_APP_ID, GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (PEM, multi-line), GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID, GITHUB_OWNER, GITHUB_REPO, GITHUB_BASE_BRANCH (default 'main'), TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. Inbound payload contract: { title: string (required), head: string (required, source branch must already exist on remote), body?: string, base?: string (defaults to GITHUB_BASE_BRANCH or 'main'), draft?: boolean, chat_id?: number|string, user_id?: number|string }. The caller is responsible for pushing 'head' before invoking this workflow; if 'head' is missing GitHub returns 422 and the error surfaces via Telegram.
